title: Deyeuxia autumnalis
intro: Deyeuxia autumnalis, also known as Autumnal Deyeuxia or Autumnal Grass-leaved Deyeuxia, is a type of perennial grass found in mountainous regions of China and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Deyeuxia autumnalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make tea, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal remedies such as tablets and capsules.
taxonomy: Deyeuxia autumnalis is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. This classification is further divided into the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. Within the tribe Poeae, Deyeuxia autumnalis is placed in the subtribe Poinae. The genus Deyeuxia is native to the southern hemisphere, particularly in South America, Australia, and New Zealand. Further classification details for Deyeuxia autumnalis may be subject to ongoing research and revisions.
